slides.title: Two conditions determine whether the program can scale

items.label: Demand
items.body: Users must experience a recurring problem with measurable urgency.

items.label: Delivery
items.body: The operating model must solve that problem reliably at attractive cost.

slides.title: Three moves convert the strategy into execution

items.label: Focus
items.body: Choose the segments where the advantage is strongest.

items.label: Build
items.body: Concentrate resources on the few capabilities that matter.

items.label: Scale
items.body: Replicate the proven model with clear owners and measures.

slides.title: Four workstreams address the full change agenda

items.label: Market
items.body: Prioritize customers and define the value proposition.

items.label: Offer
items.body: Simplify the portfolio around defensible use cases.

items.label: Operations
items.body: Redesign processes, roles, and decision rights.

items.label: Enablers
items.body: Align data, technology, talent, and governance.

slides.title: Five design choices shape the target operating model

items.label: Mandate
items.body: Define the outcome and the decisions in scope.

items.label: Structure
items.body: Set accountabilities across central and local teams.

items.label: Process
items.body: Standardize the few critical end-to-end routines.

items.label: Metrics
items.body: Track value, pace, quality, and adoption.

items.label: Cadence
items.body: Create a regular forum for decisions and escalation.

slides.title: Six capabilities create a repeatable growth engine

items.label: Insight
items.body: Translate evidence into a focused opportunity thesis.

items.label: Proposition
items.body: Match the offer to the highest-value customer job.

items.label: Channel
items.body: Reach customers through the most efficient route.

items.label: Conversion
items.body: Equip teams with simple, testable sales motions.

items.label: Retention
items.body: Use service and outcomes to reinforce loyalty.

items.label: Learning
items.body: Feed performance data back into the next cycle.

slides.title: Seven controls keep implementation on course

items.label: Review
items.body: Use evidence to decide and adapt.

slides.title: Eight questions provide a complete investment screen
Nine levers cover the transformation system without overlap
Centralization improves control, while local ownership preserves speed

left.heading: Centralize when consistency creates value
left.body: Use common standards for scarce expertise, enterprise risk, shared data, and cross-business investment decisions.

right.heading: Localize when proximity creates value
right.body: Keep customer decisions, rapid experimentation, and market-specific trade-offs close to the operating teams.

runs.text: centralize standards and platforms; localize customer-facing decisions within explicit guardrails.
